module_mood_contract: Pre-M1 logistics — no module mood contract of its own. Sets up M1 joyful creation without pre-empting it. Strategy: gift-first, peer-register, never sold as a gap-fix. Close seeds M1 Connections opener.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+/10 applies to TEACHING beats only; setup beats exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All teaching beats score ≥7 (Step 1: 7, Step 3 framing: 7, Step 4: 8, Close: 9). No teaching beat below 7. Step 0 reclassified as SETUP (launch) — exempt from mood-floor per carve-out; judges on clarity + brevity → one clear line, PASS.
Prework is pre-M1 logistics. Close seeds warm anticipation via Connections question without resolving M1's joyful-creation arc.

Step 4 is the prework's central pedagogical move — first experience of handing a multi-step task to Claude. Forcing function (one-question-at-a-time bug screening) encoded inside fenced prompt. Trace score 8. Persona: 'The agentic setup sequence is satisfying.'

No Big Idea section (logistics file). Judged against strategy 'gift first, never sold as gap-fix.' Close: 'No trick? Bring a frustration instead.' is gift-first, peer-register, non-punitive. Faithful to AE101 wizard positioning.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — prework hands off to M1.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M1 detail: 'M1 starts in the chat with the repo open; Ex1 is a read-and-introspect move.' Prework delivers: Claude Code open, repo chosen, skills installed, bug picked. Connections question seeds M1 Connections opener. Clean handoff.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No conversation cues, no pause beats, no comparison prompts, no 'share with neighbor' patterns. All body text is work-focused instructions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing function lives in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 4 forcing function ('ask one question at a time if you need to, no preamble' + multi-step agentic task structure) is encoded inside the fenced prompt block. Body prose names the move; prompt enacts it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.

rules_evaluated.evidence: <CONTENT_URL> appears in fenced blocks at lines 38 and 29. Documented exception in maintainer block: build-time substituted by scripts/build-workbook.js per-customer. Not student fill-in. Carve-out explicitly noted in maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 3.b: 'Ask Claude to fetch the content tarball to ~/Downloads.' Step 4: 'Ask Claude to extract the tarball, install the curated skills, screen three candidate bugs, and confirm the repo is ready for Module 1.' Both are semantic command-verb lead-ins under 20 words.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompt blocks follow the canonical shape with **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* label.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Step 4 prompt names 'access-control-analysis' and 'stride' without paths. No content/skills/ path leak inside fenced blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no front-running downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prework installs access-control-analysis and stride — strategy doc M3 detail confirms these are prework installs. Connections question seeds only M1 opener. No M2-M6 content front-run.
